question_text: Which law of reflection is demonstrated when a light ray hits a mirror?

options.label: A
options.text: The angle of incidence equals the angle of refraction

options.label: B
options.text: The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection

options.label: C
options.text: The incident ray bends towards the normal

options.label: D
options.text: The speed of light changes when hitting the mirror

explanation: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence (angle between incoming ray and normal) equals the angle of reflection (angle between outgoing ray and normal). The normal is an imaginary line perpendicular to the surface.
